goal: Build AgLink: one canonical workspace (single AGENTS.md, single MCP config, shared memory, cross-agent session handoff) projected across all coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, GitHub Copilot; later Cursor/Gemini/Windsurf)
summary: AgLink v0.1.0 is built, tested, and committed (2 commits on main, working tree may contain untracked .codex/ and image.png). It is a pure-Python (3.14, stdlib-only, no pip available on this machine) tool living at C:\Users\GD\Documents\AgLink that solves cross-agent config fragmentation. Phase 1 (config projector): canonical .agentsync/ workspace (AGENTS.md, mcp.json, config.toml) projected via `aglink sync` into CLAUDE.md (@import of canonical), .mcp.json, root AGENTS.md (Codex/OpenCode), .github/copilot-instructions.md, .vscode/mcp.json, and .agentsync/generated/codex.config.toml. Commands: init/sync/sync --check/status/serve/sessions. Sync refuses to clobber files it didn't generate; hash manifest (.agentsync/.aglink-manifest.json, gitignored) powers drift detection. Phase 2 (session handoff MCP server): aglink/mcpserver.py implements MCP stdio transport by hand (newline-delimited JSON-RPC 2.0; initialize/ping/tools/list/tools/call) exposing session_checkpoint, session_resume, session_list, memory_append, memory_search backed by aglink/store.py writing to .agentsync/sessions/*.json and .agentsync/memory/*.md. 12/12 end-to-end tests passed (scratchpad test_mcp.py spawned two separate server processes simulating claude->codex handoff). The server is registered in canonical mcp.json as `python -m aglink serve` and projected everywhere; it is confirmed working live (this very checkpoint was saved through it). Known Windows quirks handled: cp1252 console (stdout reconfigured to utf-8, ASCII-safe output), no symlinks (generated files with banner instead).
next_steps: Pick and build phase 3 - candidates in order discussed: MCP gateway/proxy (aggregate user's other MCP servers behind the one aglink endpoint), global machine-wide canonical layer merged with per-repo (user chose 'both, per-project first' so global layer is promised), Cursor/Gemini/Windsurf adapters, `aglink doctor` command (detect installed agents + drift)
Consider a file watcher for auto-sync on .agentsync/ edits
Consider semantic (embedding) memory search to replace keyword scoring
Package/distribute: pyproject.toml exists with `aglink` console script; user has no pip on PATH (python is Windows Store 3.14.6), so `pip install -e .` may need py -m pip or python -m pip check

decisions: Pure Python stdlib-only, NO third-party deps and NO Node - machine has no node/npm/pip; do not introduce dependencies
Canonical source of truth is .agentsync/ per-repo (git-tracked); global machine-wide layer deferred to a later phase, merging per-repo over global
CLAUDE.md is a thin @import of .agentsync/AGENTS.md (live single source), NOT a copy; other agents get copies with an AUTO-GENERATED banner
Generated files + banner instead of symlinks (Windows Dev Mode requirement)
MCP stdio protocol implemented by hand rather than the mcp SDK (no pip)
Session/memory state lives in-repo under .agentsync/ so it is git-trackable and travels with the project
Git commits: author AND committer must be Ganeshdarshan Bhat <bhatganeshdarshan10@gmail.com>, NO Co-Authored-By trailer (user explicit instruction; local git config already set)
Targets for v0.1: claude, codex, copilot (user picked; Cursor deferred)
